title: The Ataxin-2 protein is required in Kenyon cells for RNP-granule assembly and appetitive long-term memory formation
author: Hillebrand, Jens
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This publication demonstrates that the RNA binding protein Aatxin2 in the fruitfly  Drosophila melanogaster  is required for the formation of long term memory (LTM) in specific subsets of mushroom body neurons, an important structure involved in olfactory processing and memory storage. Further the requirement of Ataxin2 for the formation of RNP granules, subcellular structures involved in LTM, was shown.
steps_raw: This publication demonstrates that the RNA binding protein Aatxin2 in the fruitfly  Drosophila melanogaster  is required for the formation of long term memory (LTM) in specific subsets of mushroom body neurons, an important structure involved in olfactory processing and memory storage. Further the requirement of Ataxin2 for the formation of RNP granules, subcellular structures involved in LTM, was shown.
